hooks.description: EXAMPLE (disabled by default). A PreToolUse policy gate: denies any tool call whose arguments contain an obviously-destructive shell pattern (rm -rf, mkfs, drive format, fork bomb). A non-zero exit fails closed and blocks the call before it runs. Enable it in the UI Hooks tab (or set enabled:true here) to see the gate bite; edit scripts/example-deny-dangerous.js to encode your own policy.

EXAMPLE (disabled by default). A PostToolUse observer: runs AFTER a tool executes (advisory - it cannot un-run the call) and receives the tool result on stdin. The stub exits 0 (clean pass); replace its body to forward results to your own audit sink, metrics, or SIEM. Enable it in the UI Hooks tab (or set enabled:true here).
